title: Worked Nursing Hours by Ward and Month

landmines.name: night-shift-month-boundary
landmines.kills: naive/1_group_by_scheduled_end_month.sql -- attributing a shift to the month of scheduled_end lands a NIGHT shift that starts 29 Feb and ends 1 Mar in March instead of February

landmines.name: double-booked-duplicate
landmines.kills: naive/2_no_dedup_double_counts.sql -- SUM over raw rows counts a double-booked (duplicated) shift twice, over-stating the ward's hours

notes: Attribute each shift to the month of shift_date (the calendar date it starts on), not scheduled_end (a NIGHT shift ends the next day and can cross a month/leap boundary). Metric = SUM(COALESCE(actual_hours,0)) over each ward's nurse shifts in the month, excluding only status = 'CANCELLED'. A no-show has NULL actual_hours -> counts as 0 but must keep its ward-month present. Double-booked rows (identical business tuple, new shift_id) are collapsed with DISTINCT so they count once. reference.sql is the SQLite form (substr for the 'YYYY-MM' key); reference.postgres.sql / reference.mysql.sql / reference.duckdb.sql give the per-engine month key. mssql omitted (needs FORMAT/CONVERT for the month key; not verified in the authoring sandbox).
